subject: tensor-chain-fmax-n16
notes: kind=estimate per issue #9 text ('fmax per N from timing report, kind: estimate until hardware-run') — Quartus Timing Analyzer static-timing result, not a silicon measurement; no AXC3000 board available in this session. dsp_tensor_mode=0 is real and expected: see rtl/microbench/l0_tensor_chain/README.md — Quartus Prime Pro 26.1 refuses to elaborate the tensor-mode WYSIWYG primitive for FAMILY "Agilex 3". 80 real classic Fixed-Point [A] DSP blocks used (16 blocks x 10-wide dot product / 2 multiplies per classic DSP = 80, 58% of the device's 138-DSP budget), 0 DSP_PRIME [C] blocks — again matches the per-block 5-DSP cost exactly. scripts/audit_tensor_mode.py correctly FAILs this report against any expected tensor count > 0. Emitted manually from a real `quartus_sh --flow compile l0_tensor_chain -c l0_tensor_chain_n16` run in this session.
